pattern: A digit dynamic program over the digits of n with a carry tracks, position by position, the no-zero digits chosen for both addends and whether each addend has run out of digits.
hints: Brute force adds every a from 1 to n-1, but n can be huge. Think about building a and b one decimal digit at a time from the least significant end, with a carry between positions.
Each addend's digit at a live position must be 1..9 (never 0). The two chosen digits plus the incoming carry must match the corresponding digit of n.
The two numbers may have different lengths, so track for each addend whether it still has digits left; an addend that has ended contributes 0. Memoize on (position, carry, a-active, b-active).

edge_inputs.description: smallest n has the single pair (1,1)
pairs using 10 are excluded for n=11
n containing a zero digit constrains carries

stress.description: moderate n so the O(n) oracle stays fast while exercising multi-digit carries
stress.note: kept within range the brute oracle can enumerate quickly
